Displeased with the misdeeds of human beings, the Gods
create these portents. The king should cause propitiatory rites to be
performed in his kingdom for warding off their evil consequences.

374 Bfhat SamhitU
[The effects of both good and bad deeds are indicated by good
and bad omens respectively. These Utpatas are the precursors of
disasters. Some people argue that remedial ceremonies are quite
unnecessary and ineffective in preventing what is destined to happen
since the latter is the effect of Prarabdka i.e. deeds of previous life
that have already begun to yield their effecis. If we were to follow
this philosophy of fatalism, man would have no impetus to do any
work for improving his lot. The PrUrabdha gives man certain in-
herent tendencies, no doubt, but he, being a thinking animal, has
